page: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz, delivering 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is anchored by a 38 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7a.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only bandwidth for both small and large blocks, alongside strong write performance. The instance excels in compute-heavy tasks, registering top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing. Cryptographic and physics simulations via Passmark also rank in the top 10%. For database and memory-bound workloads, the server achieves top-tier Redis SET operations at 172.4 million operations per second and strong Passmark database performance. It also shows high efficiency in LLM inference, particularly in text generation for medium and large models.

Given its high core density and DDR5 memory speed, the c7a.24xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a deliberate tradeoff, prioritizing raw processing power and memory speed over massive memory capacity. This configuration is highly suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, software compilation, video encoding, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 96-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data places this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. The c7a.24xlarge is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, making it an excellent fit for software compilation, scientific modeling, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring a 96-core AMD EPYC processor, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.24xlarge is a 96-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized server with 192 GB DDR5 RAM and top-tier CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density, 96-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized platform featuring DDR5 memory and top-tier processing performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 cores running at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark performance
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for CPU-based machine learning inference and batch processing
